outcome: Verify that README.md and STARTUP_README.md are aligned on shared product claims, commands, and API entrypoints; make no edit unless repo-backed verification shows a concrete mismatch.
done_means: README.md and STARTUP_README.md are compared only on overlapping claims, not on completeness.
Any disputed claim is checked against the current implementation in src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py, src/ashare_harness_v2/runtime_harness/web.py, and the referenced scripts.
If a concrete inconsistency is found, the fix is minimal and limited to README.md and/or STARTUP_README.md.
If no concrete inconsistency is found, no files are changed and the result is a documented pass.
Non-goal: expanding, rewriting, or modernizing examples that are merely more detailed or date-stamped without contradicting the other doc or the repo.
review_checks: Read README.md, STARTUP_README.md, and .claude/skills/ceo-delegation-loop/SKILL.md before deciding scope.
Enumerate the shared claims: ask-stock, discover-ideas, advice/discovery APIs, primary entry guidance, and any overlapping run/serve commands.
Verify those claims against src/ashare_harness_v2/cli.py, src/ashare_harness_v2/runtime_harness/web.py, and scripts/ before editing.
Pass only if there is either no diff in the two docs or a minimal diff that resolves a proven mismatch and nothing else.
risks: README.md is a high-level summary and STARTUP_README.md is an operator guide, so extra detail in STARTUP_README.md is not by itself an inconsistency.
Hardcoded example dates and session paths may look stale but are not automatically inconsistent unless they contradict shared claims or current entrypoints.
Generated artifacts under data/output/codex_delegate may contain prior plans for the same task and should not be treated as source of truth.

work_packets.title: Audit Shared Doc Claims
work_packets.objective: Audit the two docs on their shared claims, verify each against the current repo, and apply the smallest possible doc correction only if a concrete inconsistency is confirmed.
scope: Compare README.md and STARTUP_README.md on overlapping claims only.
Check referenced CLI commands, API routes, and shell entrypoints against the implementation.
Edit only README.md and/or STARTUP_README.md if a mismatch is proven.
Do not broaden wording, refresh examples, or rewrite structure without a concrete inconsistency.
deliverables: A short pass/fail audit note that names any concrete inconsistency found, or states that the docs are aligned.
If needed, a minimal patch touching only README.md and/or STARTUP_README.md.
Verification evidence mapping each changed claim to the current CLI, API handler, or shell script.
